channel.title: Generation AI (EN)
channel.description: Latest news, events and research from the Generation AI project.

item.title: Heureka learning material: Skilled AI
item.description: Heureka has published the Skilled AI learning package, which introduces machine learning, AI ethics, and supercomputing themes for school teaching.

item.title: Heureka workshop: AI sorter with Arduino
item.description: Heureka's learning materials also include an AI sorter with Arduino workshop aimed especially at lower secondary and upper secondary education.

item.title: New tools and materials integration site!
item.description: New tools and materials integration site is now open! Welcome to explore! Community, feedback and sharing tools will be added to the site later, but even as it is, this update is a major step forward. Materials are no longer on the project website, but together with our unique AI tools designed for educational use, they form a comprehensive whole. The site can be found here: https://www.gen-ai.fi

item.title: Generation AI at EDUCA2025 event
item.description: Welcome to the EDUCA2026 event to try out our new applications, especially the small language engine. The small language engine is the world's first language model that works in a browser (no separate server needed) and can be trained with your own data. At our booth, you can also take a look at our new website (https://www.gen-ai.fi), which brings together our applications and teaching materials into one complete package! Welcome to booth 6k18 at the convention center!

item.title: The Generation AI project promoting AI literacy in children and young people has won the 2025 national Open Educational Resource Award.
item.description: The Generation AI project promoting AI literacy in children and young people has won the 2025 national Open Educational Resource Award. The award was presented on Tuesday, May 27, at the Open Science Summer Days in Helsinki. According to the award presenters, the Generation AI project is highly timely, the materials are openly available, and they are based on co-design, which is why the award for open educational material of the year was decided to be given to this project. Generation AI…

item.title: GenAI Teachable Machine is included in Microsoft's FarmBeats for Students package
item.description: Generation AI's Teachable Machine is now used as part of Microsoft's FarmBeats for Students STEM package. The Microsoft Learn module is a beginner-level 10-unit module for K-12 educators.

item.title: Generation AI at ITK2025 Event
item.description: Generation AI project greetings from ITK2025 The Generation AI project was strongly represented at the 2025 Interactive Technology in Education (ITK) event, which is the largest educational technology event in our country. ITK Forum Why is social media addictive? How does artificial intelligence work? Artificial intelligence is like a black box, its contents equally a mystery to children, young people and teachers alike. It is clear that we need tools, methods and content...

item.title: YLE UUTISPODCAST: Deepseek says it is more efficient than its competitors – are the claims true?
item.description: Prof. Teemu Roos was interviewed on YLE news podcast on 30.1.2025. Listen to the debate here: https://areena.yle.fi/podcastit/1-72885588 Deepseek, an AI company, has dispelled beliefs that China is lagging far behind in the AI race. Why does Deepseek work more efficiently than other AIs and is this as big a leap as initially thought? Sami Lindfors’…

item.title: YLE YKKÖSAAMU: interview with Prof. Roos
item.description: Prof. Teemu Roos was interviewed on YLE’s prime time programme on 29.1.2025. You can find the recording of the first morning here: https://areena.yle.fi/podcastit/1-72883304 Chinese company Deepseek has hit the AI world this week like a bolt from the blue. An AI company unveiled a new model for problem solving that is more efficient than known…

item.title: YLE AAMU: Artificial intelligence is developing faster than expected
item.description: Prof. Teemu Roos was interviewed on YLE in the morning on 29.1.2025. Watch a recording of the broadcast here: https://areena.yle.fi/1-73261994 Deepseek, a Chinese company, has shaken up the artificial intelligence market and the debate about the future of the field. This is because it is estimated that Deepseek, an AI programme launched last week, is…

item.title: Generation AI project participates in EDUCA25 event
item.description: At EDUCA, we will publish learning materials and guidelines to support the SOMEcon and the profiling game for Finnish teachers and students! We will also release an extension to the machine to be taught, making it an XAI (explainable AI) tool. In practice, the extension also makes the use of the machine being taught very…

item.title: Somekone 2.0 has been published
item.description: A second-generation version of the social media simulator we developed in our project, the SOME Machine, has already been released. For the end-user, the most visible changes include the finalised Finnish language support and more advanced network connectivity management. Teachers often use the “core concepts” version of the machine, where e.g. the link in the…

item.title: The machine to be taught was equipped with an XAI tool. Now you can see in real time how the AI classifies the image
item.description: A neural network model based on deep learning of the machine being taught was given a heat map view of the AI to be explained. It not only facilitates the use of the app by demonstrating how and what the machine recognises, but also gives students a hands-on experience of how AI works.

item.title: Publication:The data-driven comprehensive school – balancing the protection of children’s personal data with educational objectives
item.description: Data is collected almost everywhere, and schools have also become data factories.Data literacy, personal data protection and digital security skills are becoming increasingly important and a prerequisite for participation in society. The concept of datafication describes the transformation of aspects of an individual’s life into digital data that can be used for descriptive, predictive or…

item.title: The Great Nobel Debate, 24.10.2024
item.description: Our project researcher, Prof. Teemu Roos, will take part in a major Nobel debate on how machine learning and award-winning physics researchers are connected. https://acadsci.fi/ajankohtaista/tapahtumat/suuri-nobel-keskustelu-24-10-2024/—The Great Nobel Debate, 24.10.2024 Time: 24.10. 17.00-18.30Venue: Sanomatalo Mediatorium (Töölönlahdenkatu 2, 00100 Helsinki) Welcome to hear about Nobel Prize-winning research! Finnish experts tell us which research in medicine, physics, chemistry…

item.title: Speech on AI at the Eastern Finland Regional State Administrative Agency’s Rectors’ and Leadership Days 2024
item.description: Generation AI’s Professor Matti Tedre and researcher Henriikka Vartiainen will participate in the Principal and Leadership Days organised by the Regional Administrative Office of Eastern Finland and the North Karelia Rectors’ Association in Nurmes. They will give a lecture on artificial intelligence on Friday 13 September. More information about the event can be found here.

item.title: ICALT2024 best paper: a No-Code AI Education Tool for Learning AI in K-12 by Making Machine Learning-Driven Apps
item.description: Our lead programmer Nicolas Pope’s paper on a teaching machine was selected as the best paper at the IEEE’s flagship conference on educational technology: Pope, N., Vartiainen, H., Kahila, J., Laru, J., & Tedre, M. (2024). A No-Code AI Education Tool for Learning AI in K-12 by Making Machine Learning-Driven Apps. 2024 IEEE International Conference…

item.title: Itä-Savo: Kerimäki school already uses AI as part of teaching – “Aiming for personal thinking and understanding of content”
item.description: Primary school students were provided with a Teachable Machine app and learning materials to support their AI learning. I peek at the computer screen of seventh-grader Jesse Vainika and the AI recognises me as Tarja Halonen. Next to Aarne Vanhatalo and Hertta Rautiainen, who are chuckling as this happens to more people testing an AI-based…

item.title: WAIE Monthly AI Expert Webinar Series
item.description: Prof. Teemu Roos was interviewed at the Women In AI Ethics webinar on 25.4.2024. Watch the video below.

item.title: Susanna Lindroos-Hovinheimo: What did the AI regulation look like? (Constitutional Blog)
item.description: The EU AI Regulation was a long and thorough process. Now the text is ready and we can reflect on how it turned out. The first thing to say is that it turned out to be a long one. The Regulation contains 113 articles and one article is typically longer than one A4 strip. To…
